rag_rules: Keep component-type contract content in component type owners, and implementation-only bindings in component implementation owners.
Insert implementation-owned annex content after the subcomponents and connections it relies on.
Use only identifiers visible in the chosen owner; implementation-only subcomponent paths do not belong in a type owner.
Preserve the existing AADL architecture while fusing annex text; do not invent new declarations to satisfy references.
generic_rules: Edit only the intended owner and leave features, subcomponents, connections, and property associations unchanged.
Maintain owner/scope alignment between generated annex content and the AADL element that receives it.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annexes use the wrapper annex agree {** ... **};
Type owners normally hold assume/guarantee/const/eq items, while implementation owners may hold implementation bindings such as assign.
anti_patterns: Do not place guarantee clauses in an implementation annex.
Do not insert subcomponent-port references into a component type annex.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Property Names Are Not AGREE Variables
